approval.approvalNote: Approved seven-section Corvus product design in the 2026-07-16 Codex thread

shell.purpose: Open read-only workspace, profile, account, and session details without offering inline profile changes.

navigation.purpose: Open the remaining authorized routes from mobile bottom navigation and restore focus on close.

sections.purpose: Authenticate the person, explain continuity, collect presentation choices, select runtime policy, and create or join a real workspace.

copy.heading: Start with your Corvus identity
copy.continuity: Use the same governed workspace on your signed-in devices.

sections.responsiveBehavior: Desktop uses a narrow decision lane with explanatory rail; tablet centers the lane; mobile uses one full-width decision per step with persistent Back and progress text.
sections.customJustification: The sequence must separate identity, presentation, runtime, and workspace authority instead of compressing them into a generic signup form.
failureConditions: Client choices imply membership or authority
Cloud or billing claims exceed capability
Workspace data loads before runtime selection
Focus or selected state is lost

sections.purpose: Make a governed agent conversation the default product surface and keep the next useful action always reachable.

copy.composerPlaceholder: Describe the outcome, constraints, and proof you need

sections.responsiveBehavior: Desktop preserves thread history and optional inspector; tablet moves inspector to a drawer; mobile is thread-first with composer above bottom navigation.
sections.customJustification: Corvus adds provider, runtime, autonomy, approval, and audit truth to a composer-led interaction without overwhelming Everyday profiles.
failureConditions: Send or Stop state is ambiguous
Secrets enter visible or synchronized text

sections.purpose: Expose persisted run progress, approval gates, results, and evidence as one legible governed route.
sections.responsiveBehavior: Horizontal route at desktop, compact stacked route at tablet/mobile, inspector becomes a full-screen semantic dialog on mobile.
sections.customJustification: The flightpath is Corvus's unique operational signature and binds user-visible progress to authoritative events.

sections.purpose: Create and supervise unattended work with clear recurrence, readiness, authorization snapshot, and recovery policy.
sections.responsiveBehavior: Calendar and ledger share desktop space; tablet defaults to list with filter drawer; mobile uses chronological list and full-screen editor.
sections.customJustification: A time ruler and execution ledger communicate recurrence and effects more truthfully than a tile dashboard.
failureConditions: Timezone, missed-run, runtime, budget, or authorization snapshot is hidden

sections.purpose: Make account, user, device, workspace, thread, and team-policy scope explicit while safely configuring providers, MCP, integrations, and runtime defaults.
sections.responsiveBehavior: Indexed settings rail and document plane at desktop; scoped index drawer at tablet/mobile; destructive actions stay separated.
sections.customJustification: Scope and policy inheritance must remain visible beside each editable value rather than hidden in a generic settings form.
failureConditions: Scope or policy owner is unclear

sections.purpose: Coordinate assignments, reviews, approvals, comments, presence, and shared knowledge through server-returned membership capabilities.
sections.responsiveBehavior: Assignment lanes and review queue share desktop; tablet toggles queue/activity; mobile uses ordered work and review sheets.
sections.customJustification: Assignments, review, presence, and audit activity need distinct but connected structures; a member-card grid would hide authority and work state.
failureConditions: Presence is presented as audit truth

sections.purpose: Pair local nodes, choose explicit placement, supervise local/E2B lifecycle, and recover without silent authority or data-context changes.

copy.heading: Where Corvus runs
copy.cloudTruth: Cloud fallback and wake controls are not rendered until the native runtime selector, real Cloud path, and advertised lifecycle capabilities exist.

sections.responsiveBehavior: Topology plus health ledger at desktop; stacked checkpoints at tablet; text-first readiness and recovery sequence at mobile.

untilAll: runtime placement capability advertises cloud fallback
real Corvus Cloud path is enabled
an authorized Cloud runtime binding exists
real Corvus Cloud path is enabled

sections.customJustification: The topology must show control-plane ownership, node/sandbox generation, and fallback consequences without exposing credentials or raw endpoints.
failureConditions: Fallback or wake controls render before every capability gate passes
Cloud is presented without the Preview label before its real path is enabled
Cloud lifecycle or cleanup is hidden
Runtime change silently migrates or merges data
